name: command-room
summary: A captain's or warlord's strategy room — a central map table, marked pieces, banners of allied forces on the walls, correspondence stacked at a desk.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: square or rectangular — the table is the center, walls hold references

primary_palette.note: classic war room — central table with officers' chairs
working command room — planning table and correspondence desk both present
confederacy — multiple faction banners on the walls around the table
drill-room style — a sand-table miniature of the battlefield
wizardly command — table for mundane ops, mirror for divination

story_prompts: active planning session — pieces on the map, wine half-drunk, the captain just stepped out
abandoned command room, the battle was lost, papers still scattered
a traitor's command room — false orders drafted, real ones hidden
a pirate captain's chart room on a landlocked dungeon level, stolen maps pinned everywhere
an ancient command room, the war it planned over for centuries but the map still showing troop positions
anti_patterns: don't let the map-table go near a wall — it needs access on all sides
don't underlight; officers need to read the map
avoid dormitory or torture elements; this is a staff room
